key_facts: Google Cloud WAN was announced at Google Cloud Next '25 on April 9, 2025 in Las Vegas, as a headline infrastructure announcement alongside Ironwood TPU and Gemini 2.5
Cloud WAN makes Google's own global private network available to external enterprise customers for the first time in 25 years
Cloud WAN is a fully managed enterprise WAN backbone positioned as a replacement for traditional MPLS and complex SD-WAN deployments
Google's network infrastructure spans 2 million+ miles of lit fiber, 33 subsea cables, 202 network edge locations, 42 cloud regions, and 127 cloud zones
Cloud WAN claims up to 40% faster performance compared to public internet and up to 40% TCO savings vs customer-managed WAN solutions
Cloud WAN has a 99.99% uptime SLA backed by Google's backbone network
The network's WAN bandwidth increased 7x between 2020 and 2025 via a multi-shard horizontal architecture

Key components include Cloud Interconnect (159 locations), Cross-Cloud Interconnect (21 locations), Cross-Site Interconnect (Layer 2, first among major clouds), Network Connectivity Center, NCC Gateway, and Premium Tier network
Launch customers include Nestlé and Citadel Securities; Nestlé reported up to 40% application performance improvement
Partner ecosystem includes SD-WAN vendors (Cisco, Fortinet, Juniper, Palo Alto, etc.), SSE providers (Broadcom, Palo Alto Prisma Access), and GSIs (Accenture, HCLTech, Wipro)

Other major networking announcements at Next '25 included 400G Interconnect (4x bandwidth), 30,000 GPU cluster support, 3.2 Tbps RDMA networking, GKE Inference Gateway, DNS Armor, and inline Network DLP
NCC Gateway (cloud-native SSE integration) entered preview in Q2 2025
Cross-Site Interconnect provides dedicated L2 10/100G private connectivity between data centers — was in preview at announcement, later reached GA with 89 locations
In December 2025, AWS and Google Cloud jointly announced AWS Interconnect — multicloud for direct private cross-cloud connectivity, which reached GA on April 15, 2026
Cloud WAN was announced in Sundar Pichai's keynote and was part of 229 total announcements made at Next '25
